name: Saldaña Castle and Virgen del Valle Sanctuary Trail
name_original: Ruinas del Castillo de los Condes Saldaña — circular por los prados de Saldaña
description: Hike a moderate 7.7-mile (12.4 km) circular route around Saldaña, exploring historic castle ruins and scenic meadows.
region_intro: Hiking around Saldaña offers routes through varied landscapes, characterized by the Carrión River, agricultural plains, and gentle hills. The region features paths that explore local viewpoints and historical sites, providing a mix of natural and cultural experiences. Elevation gains are generally modest, making many trails accessible. The terrain primarily consists of riverine paths and open countryside. Best hiking trails around Saldaña The most popular hiking route is Trail La vuelta del Merendero — desde Saldaña, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This easy route features gentle gradients through local countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Saldaña Castle and Virgen del Valle Sanctuary Trail, a moderate 7.7 miles (12.4 km) path. This trail explores historical landmarks and offers views over the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Route along the Carrión River promenade — circular from Saldaña, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 19 minutes. Hiking around Saldaña is defined by river paths, agricultural plains, and local viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate trails with some elevation. The routes in Saldaña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Saldaña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Saldaña?
A: Saldaña offers a selection of 8 hiking routes, catering to various preferences. The majority, 6 of these, are considered easy, while 2 are moderate in difficulty.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Saldaña?
A: Yes, Saldaña is well-suited for beginners and families, with 6 out of 8 trails classified as easy. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309408854/route-along-the-carrion-river-promenade-circular-from-saldana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route along the Carrión River promenade — circular from Saldaña&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.2 miles (5.1 km) long and follows the river through gentle landscapes.

Q: Are there any moderate hiking trails for more experienced hikers in Saldaña?
A: For those seeking a moderate challenge, Saldaña offers 2 such trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070144676/saldana-castle-and-virgen-del-valle-sanctuary-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saldaña Castle and Virgen del Valle Sanctuary Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a notable option, covering 7.7 miles (12.4 km) and exploring historical landmarks with some elevation.

Q: What is the typical length of hiking trails around Saldaña?
A: The hiking trails around Saldaña vary in length. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309524620/valcabadillo-viewpoint-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcabadillo Viewpoint Loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 2.6 miles (4.2 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070144676/saldana-castle-and-virgen-del-valle-sanctuary-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saldaña Castle and Virgen del Valle Sanctuary Trail&lt;/a&gt; extends to 7.7 miles (12.4 km). Most trails fall within this range, offering options for shorter strolls or longer excursions.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Saldaña?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309408854/route-along-the-carrion-river-promenade-circular-from-saldana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route along the Carrión River promenade — circular from Saldaña&lt;/a&gt;, which is a pleasant loop along the river.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I see while hiking in Saldaña?
A: Many trails in Saldaña incorporate local viewpoints and historical sites. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saldaña Historic Town Center and Castle Ruins&lt;/a&gt;, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6122281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcabadillo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070144676/saldana-castle-and-virgen-del-valle-sanctuary-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saldaña Castle and Virgen del Valle Sanctuary Trail&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights these types of landmarks.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Saldaña?
A: The hiking routes in Saldaña are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Hikers often appreciate the varied landscapes, from riverine paths to open countryside, and the blend of natural beauty with historical points of interest.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Saldaña?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking in Saldaña. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery, whether blooming or displaying autumnal colors, is particularly appealing. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, but be prepared for warmer conditions.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Saldaña?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the hiking trails around Saldaña, especially those through open countryside and along river paths. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, particularly near agricultural areas or wildlife, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage on individual routes.

Q: Is parking available near the hiking trailheads in Saldaña?
A: Yes, parking is typically available in or near Saldaña town center, which serves as a common starting point for many routes. For trails that begin further afield, you can often find designated parking areas in nearby villages or at specific points of interest. It's recommended to check the tour details on komoot for specific parking information for your chosen route.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Saldaña using public transport?
A: Saldaña is a small town, and while some regional bus services connect it to larger cities, direct public transport to specific trailheads outside the town center may be limited. Many trails are accessible directly from Saldaña itself, making it convenient if you arrive by bus. For routes further out, a car might be more practical.

Q: What kind of gear or clothing should I wear for hiking in Saldaña?
A: Given the varied terrain of riverine paths and open countryside, comfortable walking shoes or light hiking boots are recommended. Layers of clothing are advisable to adapt to changing weather conditions, especially during spring and autumn. Don't forget sun protection, water, and a map or GPS device, such as the komoot app, for navigation.
tips: Palencia town with a history closely related to the counts of Saldaña whose castle, although in ruins, can be visited.
Fortification of medieval origin that is in ruins. It is located on top of a hill next to the town of Saldaña offering privileged views of the town and its surroundings.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: While the trail is rated as moderate, its paths are generally accessible. It requires a good level of fitness due to its length and some elevation changes, but it can be suitable for families with active children or beginners looking for a bit of a challenge.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring easily accessible paths. You'll traverse open, grassy areas (the 'prados de Saldaña') and encounter a clay hill around the castle. The castle itself was built with limestone masonry and rubble fill, indicating some rocky sections near the ruins.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is undoubtedly the Ruins of the Castle of the Counts of Saldaña, a medieval fortress with significant historical importance. You'll also pass by the Church of San Pedro, Saldaña, and enjoy the rural beauty of the Saldaña-Valdavia region, including views of the Carrión River often flanked by poplar groves.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Saldaña. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the town of Saldaña, near the starting point of the loop, to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to be mindful of other hikers and local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this circular trail or to visit the castle ruins. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is during spring or autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures, lush landscapes, and avoid the intense heat of summer or the potentially muddy conditions of winter. Spring brings blooming meadows, while autumn showcases beautiful fall colors.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several local paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Camino a la Virgen del Valle, Camino de la morterona, LG Raposeras, Calle de la Puentecilla, and Calle San Lázaro.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restaurants or cafes nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the trail starts and ends in Saldaña, you'll find various facilities within the town. There are restaurants, cafes, and bars where you can refuel before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque views of the rural Saldaña-Valdavia region, characterized by its agricultural fields and livestock. The trail offers scenic perspectives of the Carrión River and, most notably, panoramic views of the historic Ruins of the Castle of the Counts of Saldaña perched on its clay hill.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, wear comfortable hiking shoes suitable for walking on paths and potentially uneven ground. Bring water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing to adapt to changing weather conditions. A camera is also recommended for the scenic views.

waypoints.name: Ruins of the Castle of the Counts of Saldaña
